faqs.question: Why are drain flies common in Sutton Place apartments?
faqs.answer: Drain flies are particularly common in Sutton Place due to the area's many pre-war buildings with aging plumbing systems and the high-density nature of Manhattan co-ops. These flies breed in the organic matter that accumulates in drains, garbage disposals, and sewer lines. The moisture-rich environment of bathroom and kitchen drains in older buildings creates ideal breeding conditions.

faqs.question: How long does drain fly treatment take in a Sutton Place apartment?
faqs.answer: Most drain fly treatments in Sutton Place apartments take 1-2 hours depending on the size of your unit and the severity of the infestation. Our technicians will inspect all drains, apply bio-enzymatic treatments to break down organic matter, and may use additional methods like foam treatments or growth regulators. Follow-up visits are sometimes necessary for complete elimination.

faqs.question: What's included in the $350 - $550 drain fly treatment service?
faqs.answer: Our drain fly treatment includes a thorough inspection of all drains and potential breeding sites, identification of the infestation source, application of professional-grade bio-enzymatic cleaners, mechanical cleaning when needed, and treatment of all affected drains. The price varies based on apartment size, number of drains, and infestation severity. We also provide prevention recommendations to keep drain flies from returning.

faqs.question: Are drain fly treatments safe for Sutton Place luxury apartments and residents?
faqs.answer: Yes, our drain fly treatments are completely safe for occupied apartments and won't damage your plumbing or fixtures. We use EPA-approved bio-enzymatic products that break down organic matter naturally without harsh chemicals. Our methods are safe for children, pets, and seniors, making them ideal for Sutton Place's residential buildings. You can remain in your apartment during and immediately after treatment.
